address: 61 Little Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia
comment:
If you love sandwiches and are the type who would wait for 20-40 minutes to queue amd get your food, yes absolutely.
Firstly, what I like about their sandwich (and I ordered their hot salami) was their bread. Very spongy, bouncy but soft. Will eat that alone with some balsamic vinegar and olive oil. Secondly, the portion is HUGE. Not sure compared to the price, but I was struggling to finish mine.
Unfortunately personally for me, there are more downsides. Since the bread was soaking the juices, the sandwich was super soggy in the 5 minute walk I had to do, and a lot worsened by the end of my meal.
The place is so tiny. Their ordering system, considering that they have been opened for more than a year, something needed to be done with that ordering system. I constantly heard people being told to exit the store too. Well if you are getting called inside (in the first instance), your first instinct is to stay inside. I understand, OH&S, but that does mean there is a problem with the ordering and pick up system.
Their service, well ok, the two ladies who served, not the greatest but to me ok (my colleague who was with me disagreed and thought one of them was so rude to me and her).
I had chips that looked like they were cooked 2 hours before and kept warm the entire time. Flavour, meh ok, not memorable.
My sandwich had a layer of rocket leaves, their sauce which was quite sour and tangy, salamis and the chilli relish. Flavour was ok, would have made it slightly differently myself, but ok regardless.
The day before, another colleague had half of her salad sandwich left which I had. It was also just average, the extreme tangy and sour tone did not make it taste balanced at all. Vegetables were cruchy, especially the beetroot.
Will i go back and try again? Very reluctant, only if there is no line out the door. And I will likely try their mushroom melt as my colleague said that hers was quite good.

address: 253 Coventry St, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia
comment:
Hector’s Deli in South Melbourne is one of my favourite sandwich spots and the place where I first discovered the brand a few years ago. Their coffee is excellent, but the real star of the show here is definitely the sandwiches.

My go to order is the beef and pickles and I always add their signature “hectic” sauce. It is seriously elite. The sandwiches are packed with fresh ingredients and always incredibly satisfying.

I also really like their breakfast muffins. They remind me of a much fancier version of a McDonald’s sausage and egg McMuffin, and of course I add the hectic sauce to that as well.

This was also the location where I first tried their original maple glazed donuts, which are so good.

Over the years I have also tried the mushroom melt, tuna melt and the side salad, but the beef and pickles is still my favourite.

Service is always super friendly and efficient. There is a small amount of seating inside and some outdoor seating as well, but these days there is almost always a line of people waiting for takeaway which tells you how popular it has become.

I will be honest, these are not cheap sandwiches. If the idea of spending $15 to $20 on a sandwich makes you uncomfortable then this might not be the place for you. That said, I genuinely think they are worth every dollar and I would highly recommend trying it at least once.

I am also a bit of a sucker for their branding. It manages to feel both minimalist and slightly retro at the same time, and they really nail that balance.

Overall it is a consistently great spot for a seriously good sandwich.

Food was fresh however people wouldn't let grandmother sit and were hogging chairs simply because they were chatting. We eventually had to go to local park to sit and eat. Looked like hipster place. Wasn't really acknowledged when they were asking if anyone is waiting on orders. Coffee spritz had a tang to it. Probably wouldn't be back but it was worth the experience.

address: 253 Coventry St, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia
comment:
I waited 30 minutes today for just one sandwich. While I was waiting, I saw someone who ordered well after me receive their food first. When I asked staff to check my order, I was told it was “not ready yet.”

When I asked again later, the sandwich bag I was given didn’t even have an order ticket attached and with fries that I never ordered. After that, I heard a staff member call my name again much later.

I don’t think the staff were rude or unprofessional. Of course, the situation could have been handled better but the real problem seems to be staffing. Running such a busy shop on a Sunday at 12pm with so few employees feels unreasonable. The company should schedule more staff.

address: 253 Coventry St, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia
comment:
This is no longer the nice place it was at its debut but rather an overhyped instagram sandwich shop , or tourist trap, that has lost interest in the customer.

First I realised that queuing and paying does not guarantee having food to eat. It is busy and will sell out, so it does not need to process all the orders. It will refund you eventually if you are patient enough to wait for it.

I am a local , going to that place from its beginning, and I am really disappointed to what it has become,so I can only imagine what I would feel if I was a tourist on a tight schedule.

I queued to get my order made and waited 1h to get … a refund. My order was lost and the food sold out. Now I understand the concept of mistake, but, to the person at the counter at the end of service:
- when asked to check the status of an order, turning your head to the kitchen and saying “yeah, it’s coming “ is not good enough. You have to track the order. You would have done that before all that hype.
- when after 1h of waiting, offering a refund for the sandwich is not enough. You have to apologise also because you cannot pay back the time that person has spent waiting.

I am a bit heartbroken because it was the kind of place that used to be good before being well known. I would recommend other establishments, which are still humbly doing the job.

address: 253 Coventry St, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia
comment:
Nice little spot with a great vibe, though it was very busy when we went.

The sandwiches were amazing, especially the bread, which really made them stand out. We ordered a tuna melt and a mushroom melt and both were very tasty with good quality ingredients. Also grabbed a choc chip walnut cookie which was a nice extra treat. A few items were already sold out before 1:30pm, which shows how popular it is.

Only small improvement would be the ordering process, they take names, and when it’s busy it can get a bit confusing with multiple people having the same name. Using order numbers instead (or alongside names) could make things a lot clearer.

Overall, definitely worth the visit.

address: 253 Coventry St, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia
comment:
I recently stopped by Hector's Deli and honestly… it might have ruined all other sandwiches for me.

First of all, the place has that perfect casual Melbourne vibe—busy, a bit chaotic, but in the fun “you know the food is going to be good” kind of way. The smell alone when you walk in should probably be bottled and sold as perfume.

The sandwich itself? Absolutely stacked. Fresh bread, super generous fillings, and everything perfectly balanced. You can tell they care about the ingredients, but they’re not being pretentious about it. It’s just a really, really good sandwich done right.

Service was quick even though it was packed, and the staff were friendly and efficient. You grab your food, find a spot outside, and suddenly life feels pretty good.

My only complaint: I spent the rest of the day thinking about that sandwich and wondering if it would be socially acceptable to go back for another one immediately. (For research purposes, of course.)

If you’re in South Melbourne and like sandwiches—even a little—this place is 100% worth the stop. Just be warned: you may start comparing every future sandwich to Hector’s… and most of them will lose. 🥪

Honestly one of the most disappointing food experiences I’ve had in a long time. I ordered the chicken and it was absolutely terrible. It was way too fatty and just dripping with oil. Every bite felt like it had been soaked in grease rather than properly cooked.

The flavor wasn’t any better either. It honestly tasted like someone crushed up a packet of salt and vinegar chips and dumped it on wet chicken. Just overpowering, weird, and completely unpleasant.

Then there was the almond croissant. That thing was a disaster. The top was rock hard like it had been sitting out all day, and the inside was cold. Not even slightly warm just cold and stale.

For a place that gets so much hype, this was a massive letdown. Definitely not worth the money or the reputation people give it.

I was surprised to see a Hector’s Deli kiosk pop up on Bourke Street right outside the H&M store this week. I absolutely love their other locations in Little Collins, South Melbourne and the bigger one in Richmond with all the pastries, so I had to stop when I spotted it.

Even though it was only 10am I could not resist grabbing a maple glazed donut and it did not disappoint. So good.

This kiosk seems to focus just on pastries rather than the sandwiches they are known for, which makes it more of a quick grab and go stop. The pastries looked great and everything felt very on brand for Hector’s.

My only suggestion would be to offer some kind of coffee option as well. Even something simple like canned iced lattes would be perfect because I definitely would have grabbed one with the donut.

Still a great little addition to the CBD and a dangerous discovery for anyone who already loves Hector’s like I do.
